title: Hieracium subsilvaticum
intro: Hieracium subsilvaticum is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion-like hawkweed genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and woolly leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium subsilvaticum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium subsilvaticum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and is used to treat various conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Hieracium subsilvaticum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is a large and diverse group of plants, with over 600 species. These plants are typically found in temperate regions and are known for their yellow or white flowers. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Hieracium subsilvaticum is closely related to other flowering plants such as daisies and sunflowers.
